video_title: Flash Trade Is Reinventing Perps on Solana | Anas Khader
gist: Anas Khader of Flash Trade explains why the protocol's pool-to-peer, asset-backed perps model on Solana lets it offer 500x+ leverage across crypto, equities, commodities and forex without per-market liquidity fragmentation, and why its new V2 engine offloads order matching to a Magic Block 'co-processor' to beat on-chain latency while keeping custody and finality on Solana.

insights.claim: Flash Trade uses a pool-to-peer market-making model rather than a central limit orderbook because one shared pool can serve hundreds of markets without needing dedicated market makers and fragmented liquidity per market.
insights.speaker: Anas Khader
insights.so_what: Investors evaluating perp DEXs should weigh pool-to-peer scalability (many markets, one liquidity source) against orderbook depth when assessing which model wins long-tail and cross-asset markets.
insights.quote: The problem with uh clubs is like for every market you need a subset of market makers and then liquidity for that market. So it does not scale.

insights.claim: Anas Khader argues orderbook perp designs have little remaining design space beyond lowering latency and decentralizing, whereas pool-to-peer engines still have substantial unexplored mechanism design.
insights.speaker: Anas Khader
insights.so_what: Frames where future protocol-level innovation and differentiation may accrue, favoring pool-based DEXs over commoditized orderbook venues.
insights.quote: if you look at the club mechanism, there is not much design system left

insights.claim: Orderbooks cannot practically offer more than ~50-100x leverage on volatile assets because a 5-10 bip spread move instantly liquidates a 500x position, whereas pool-to-peer engines can offer 500x+.
insights.speaker: Anas Khader
insights.so_what: Explains the structural reason high-leverage retail demand may migrate to pool-based venues rather than orderbook DEXs.
insights.quote: you can't do more than 100x leverage on order books. You can't do 500x leverage because there is you know imagine if there is some sort of uh uh spread and if it increases more than like five bips or 10 bips you instantly get liquidated when you are filled at 500x leverage

insights.claim: Flash Trade's flagship FLP1 liquidity pool (Solana, Bitcoin, Ethereum) is up ~45% since inception versus an equivalent spot index that would be down more than 60% over the same period, driven by market-making fees.
insights.speaker: Anas Khader
insights.so_what: Suggests LPing in a pool-to-peer perp pool can materially outperform holding the underlying basket, a yield/exposure case worth diligencing against drawdown risk.
insights.quote: if you were holding these assets and in same index you would be down by more than 60% today since the FLP1 started. But if you compare with where FLP1 is, it's actually up by 45%

insights.claim: Flash Trade claims to be the first venue to offer equities, commodities and forex perp trading on Solana, using tokenized real assets like an xStocks SPY product and gold with roughly $3 billion of liquidity behind it.
insights.speaker: Anas Khader
insights.so_what: Signals on-chain perps expanding beyond crypto into TradFi asset classes, a growth vector for tokenized RWA flows on Solana.
insights.quote: we were the first ones to offer equities, commodities and forex trading per trading on Salana

insights.claim: Flash Trade's equities pool holds only SPY and USDC, but because SPY indexes all 500 companies it can be used as collateral to trade perps on those individual constituents, with LPs earning fees and P&L from equity traders.
insights.speaker: Anas Khader
insights.so_what: A capital-efficient design pattern for offering many equity markets from a single index-collateralized pool, relevant to RWA perp scaling.
insights.quote: you can always back use SPY as collateral to trade those 500 companies on burps

insights.claim: Flash Trade describes itself as an asset-backed exchange where every perpetual position is collateralized by the real underlying asset rather than USDC, so winning positions can always be paid out without auto-deleveraging during extreme moves.
insights.speaker: Anas Khader
insights.so_what: Asset-backed collateral reduces ADL/socialized-loss risk that hits USDC-backed orderbook venues in volatility, a differentiator for traders worried about payout reliability.
insights.quote: every uh perpetual position that we have live on flash is actually backed by that asset itself

insights.claim: Flash Trade listed Zcash with 50x leverage right before its roughly 200% rally, and its liquidity pool profited from Zcash even as the asset rallied then fell about 80%.
insights.speaker: Anas Khader
insights.so_what: Demonstrates the pool can offer high leverage on volatile new listings while LPs net profit from fees and trader losses regardless of direction.
insights.quote: we listed Zcash right before it went up to the rally of 200% that it jumped off all the while offering 50x leverage

insights.claim: Flash Trade's V2 engine offloads order matching, liquidations and trigger orders to a Magic Block 'co-processor' (which Anas frames as a GPU to Solana's CPU) while keeping asset custody and finality on Solana, avoiding bridging of user funds.
insights.speaker: Anas Khader
insights.so_what: A blueprint for high-performance on-chain perps that sidesteps L2 bridge risk; watch Magic Block adoption as Solana perp infrastructure.
insights.quote: the custody is on Salana the execution is on this co-processor and we are not moving your coins

insights.claim: Anas Khader argues distributed validator boxes will never match centralized exchange performance because the world's largest venues (NASDAQ, CME, Binance) co-locate at a single box that market makers run to, since liquidity follows the trusted box.
insights.speaker: Anas Khader
insights.so_what: Implies fully decentralized matching is structurally disadvantaged, supporting hybrid single-sequencer designs as the practical path for serious on-chain trading.
insights.quote: all of these boxes live at one place be it NASDAQ or CME or Binance or any other big exchange the box is at one place and the market makers run to that one box just because the liquidity is there. Liquidity is the mode.

insights.claim: Solana's effective transaction latency ranges 400-800 milliseconds rather than the headline 400ms, and even a 200ms target leaves roughly a 100x to 1000x gap versus NASDAQ's picosecond-level latency.
insights.speaker: Anas Khader
insights.so_what: Quantifies why latency-sensitive perp matching needs off-chain co-processing today and where Solana must improve to host serious HFT.
insights.quote: there is an inherent latency in the blockchain of about 400 to 800 milliseconds

insights.claim: Solana's per-account fee structure and priority fees impose heavy costs on market makers, who may run 100+ accounts and bursting 2,000 orders per second while earning only 0.5-2 bips per trade, eroding their economics at scale.
insights.speaker: Anas Khader
insights.so_what: Explains the cost pressure pushing matching off-chain and why MM economics constrain native on-chain orderbooks on Solana.
insights.quote: imagine if you are a market maker who are bursting out $2,000 uh 2,000 orders per second. Even if you're paying a penny, that scales up a lot over a month

insights.claim: The Magic Block co-processor includes an escape hatch and fraud-proof system that lets the full state be returned to Solana in the next slot and lets anyone independently challenge the final state.
insights.speaker: Anas Khader
insights.so_what: The security/decentralization backstop investors should verify when assessing co-processor designs that move execution off the base chain.
insights.quote: there is an escape hatch in the system which is very neatly designed where you can bring back the whole state to Solana back to Salana in the next round round slot

insights.claim: Flash Trade claims effectively zero slippage on a $1M SOL position at under ~6 bips of fees, versus Hyperliquid where the same trade incurs 10-15 bips of slippage that widens further during volatility.
insights.speaker: Anas Khader
insights.so_what: A concrete execution-cost edge claim to verify; if real, it attracts size traders away from orderbook venues during volatile regimes.
insights.quote: even now if you wanted to take a million dollar so long you can get it on uh flash trade with less than there is less than six bits of uh fees

insights.claim: Flash Trade's V2 beta charges 2 bips per perp trade, which Anas says is roughly 50% lower than Hyperliquid and Phoenix, and the design could eventually move to zero fees as pool P&L alone covers market makers.
insights.speaker: Anas Khader
insights.so_what: Fee compression toward zero is a competitive lever; signals margin pressure across perp DEXs as scale grows.
insights.quote: we are offering per at just two bits which is almost 50% lesser than what Hyperlid is offering and even Phoenix is offering

insights.claim: Anas Khader sees high-leverage permissionless perps absorbing the speculative retail appetite previously expressed through memecoins, noting Hyperliquid's growth shows demand for permissionless, KYC-free leveraged trading.
insights.speaker: Anas Khader
insights.so_what: Frames high-leverage perps as a substitute for memecoin gambling, suggesting where Solana retail flow rotates next.

insights.claim: Flash Trade relies on Pyth oracles with many publishers and multiple per-asset thresholds for listing, and Anas Khader sits on the Pyth council for getting feeds onboarded.
insights.speaker: Anas Khader
insights.so_what: Oracle quality is the core attack surface for pool-based perps; Pyth dependence is a key risk and diligence item.
insights.quote: the engineers that have built PET directly come from the world of Stratfire who used to work from Jump or Citadel

insights.claim: Flash Trade enforces price-bound guardrails (e.g. disallowing a Bitcoin move greater than ~30% within a 2-3 second window) and rate-limited withdrawals such as capping withdrawals at ~$200,000 at once from a ~$10M pool to verify legitimate P&L.
insights.speaker: Anas Khader
insights.so_what: These circuit breakers limit oracle-manipulation blast radius but introduce latency and centralized operator verification, a tradeoff to assess.
insights.quote: imagine the pool is at about $10 million. You can't withdraw more than $200,000 at once

insights.claim: Anas Khader argues most DeFi exploits stem from the human factor rather than smart contracts, and DeFi teams should adopt corporate practices like isolated, locked-down devices for sensitive operations.
insights.speaker: Anas Khader
insights.so_what: Reframes perp-protocol risk assessment toward operational security and key management rather than just contract audits.
insights.quote: the main thing that we have seen is like it's about the human factor that is failing a lot, not the main contracts

themes.theme: Next markets and Solana network bet

insights.claim: Anas Khader identifies 24/7 markets and pre-IPO/private-company perp markets as the two emergent products that will scale perps to the next trillion dollars of open interest.
insights.speaker: Anas Khader
insights.so_what: Points to where new perp volume and product launches are headed; pre-IPO perps are a watch item around major listings.
insights.quote: 24 by7 markets is the deal breaker with pre-markets as well where essentially you are allowing people to trade any private company that is not public

insights.claim: The host notes SpaceX may IPO within weeks and that OpenAI and Anthropic have reportedly submitted confidential IPO filings, framing potentially the three largest IPOs in history for summer 2026 as prime pre- and post-listing perp markets.
insights.speaker: host
insights.so_what: Flags specific catalysts for new equity and pre-IPO perp markets; relevant for venues racing to list these names.
insights.quote: both OpenAI and Enthropic have both submitted their confidential filings for um an eventual IPO as well

insights.claim: Anas Khader is betting on Solana getting faster and cheaper (targeting ~200ms latency) while staying decentralized, citing the Alpenglow upgrade due later in summer 2026 as a catalyst that unlocks more protocol design space.
insights.speaker: Anas Khader
insights.so_what: Flash Trade's roadmap is leveraged to Solana base-layer performance gains; Alpenglow is a network catalyst to track.
insights.quote: we are yet to see how Alpenlow comes out later this summer

insights.claim: Anas Khader says Solana's reliability has transformed from a horrible state two years ago (RPC issues, fee spikes, downtime) to smooth operation today, crediting open-source data infrastructure providers Triton and Helius.
insights.speaker: Anas Khader
insights.so_what: Improved Solana infra reliability lowers operational risk for building latency-sensitive financial apps, supporting the ecosystem investment thesis.
insights.quote: today we are not seeing any of those. The RPC network is smooth.
